hypothesis: When the GraphSAGE framework is extended to three-or-more component gas mixtures (e.g., Ar/O₂/CF₄), the predictive accuracy of mixture etching rates will degrade more slowly with increasing mixture complexity when node features are drawn from intermediate hidden layers of the pre-trained single-gas networks rather than from their output layers, because intermediate representations encode transferable plasma-chemistry embeddings that capture synergistic inter-species interactions more completely than scalar etching-rate outputs, and this advantage will be measurable as a statistically significant difference in R² between the two feature-extraction strategies across mixtures of three versus two components.
